 {"id":"bd-vj3","title":"Perf: Per-kernel microbench suite (criterion groups + thresholds)","description":"Add a criterion microbench suite that measures *per-kernel* costs, not just end-to-end decide().

## Why
End-to-end `decide()` can hide regressions. Per-kernel benches let us pin blame when a new kernel or refactor shifts cost.

## Bench Design
- Criterion group per kernel (or per family of kernels).
- Measure:
  - `decide` contributions (hot modules)
  - cadence update costs (epoch modules)
- Report ns/op and p95/p99 where applicable.

## Dependency
- Must reuse the baseline harness from `bd-d5l` and regression thresholds from `bd-242`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A new kernel cannot land without adding/adjusting its microbench.
- Thresholds catch >X% regressions (configurable) in CI.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightMoose","created_at":"2026-02-09T22:03:30.833286241Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:48.417613055Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:39.911611946Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-242","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-d5l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vj3","depends_on_id":"bd-pt6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:33Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1147,"issue_id":"bd-vj3","author":"BrightMoose","text":"Added per-kernel microbench binary crates/glibc-rs-bench/benches/runtime_math_kernels_bench.rs (machine-readable RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines) and wired it in crates/glibc-rs-bench/Cargo.toml.

Also hardened scripts/perf_gate.sh for dev environments:
- skip under extreme system load (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_SKIP_OVERLOADED=1, GLIBC_RUST_PERF_MAX_LOAD_FACTOR=0.85)
- optional kernel-suite run flag (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE=1) collecting RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines.

Baseline comparisons for kernel-suite metrics not yet enforced (needs baseline schema + stable host load to record p50s).","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:32:28Z"},{"id":1148,"issue_id":"bd-vj3","author":"CobaltCompass","text":"Verified complete by CobaltCompass. BrightMoose delivered per-kernel bench with 6 criterion groups: risk_upper_bound_ppm, bandit_select_profile, barrier_admissible, pareto_recommend_profile, design_choose_plan, approachability_observe/summary. Machine-readable RUNTIME_MATH_KERNEL_BENCH lines for CI gating. Wired into Cargo.toml and perf_gate.sh (GLIBC_RUST_PERF_ENABLE_KERNEL_SUITE=1). Note: per-kernel baseline enforcement deferred to stable host load recording.","created_at":"2026-02-11T01:27:48Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-vq1g","title":"D.1: (SUPERSEDED) — folded into parent bd-9dq5","description":"## Goal
Implement the request-tracking infrastructure for async DNS and the gai_error/gai_cancel functions.

## Design

### Request Registry
A global mutex-protected HashMap<usize, AsyncDnsRequest> maps gaicb pointer addresses to request state. The gaicb struct (defined by POSIX) contains:
- ar_name: hostname to resolve
- ar_service: service name/port
- ar_request: hints (addrinfo struct)
- ar_result: output addrinfo list (written on completion)

### gai_error(req: *mut gaicb) -> c_int
1. Look up req address in registry
2. If not found: return EAI_SYSTEM (unknown request)
3. Return state: EAI_INPROGRESS (still running), 0 (completed successfully), EAI_CANCELLED, or the error code from getaddrinfo

### gai_cancel(req: *mut gaicb) -> c_int
1. Look up req in registry
2. If not found: return EAI_NOCANCEL
3. If already completed: return EAI_ALLDONE
4. If in progress: set CANCELLED flag, return EAI_CANCELLED (or EAI_NOTCANCELED if thread already started resolution)

### State Constants
- EAI_INPROGRESS = -100 (glibc value)
- EAI_CANCELLED = -101
- EAI_ALLDONE = -103
- EAI_NOTCANCELED = -102

## Implementation
File: crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs (near existing gai_* functions)
- Add AsyncDnsRequest struct
- Add static ASYNC_DNS_REGISTRY: LazyLock<Mutex<HashMap<usize, AsyncDnsRequest>>>
- Replace gai_error body
- Replace gai_cancel body

## Testing
- gai_error on unknown request returns EAI_SYSTEM
- gai_cancel on unknown request returns EAI_NOCANCEL
- Register a fake completed request, gai_error returns 0
- Register a fake in-progress request, gai_cancel returns EAI_CANCELLED

## Acceptance Criteria
State machine is correct. Registry tracks requests. Tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:19:44.987634072Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.338086567Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T01:59:38.337881268Z","close_reason":"Superseded: Track D redesigned as synchronous-only. No request registry or futex wait needed. All logic fits in parent bd-9dq5.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async","resolv","stub-elimination"]}
